$comment: AOC v1.1 R4 — canonical schema for verify-report.md frontmatter. Closes #1056. Catches the producer-vs-consumer drift class for frontmatter (analogous to AOC v1 R3 for the fix-ledger). The check_frontmatter_artifact_consistency function in verify-linter.sh enforces (a) the writer at state-7a-write-report.md emits every declared field, and (b) every consumer references only declared field names.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC; emitted by state-7a from the run's verify-context timestamp.

build_attempts.description: Build attempts in 1..3 range. Sourced from .runs/verify-context.json.build_attempts.

fix_log_entries.description: AOC v1 FLS v1 authoritative count = wc -l .runs/fix-ledger.jsonl. Drift between this number and the ledger is a #1048 regression — R4 cross-checks at consumer time; R3 cross-checks at writer time.

agents_expected.description: Agents expected to participate per scope (from skill manifest). Used by lifecycle-finalize.sh and verify-pr-gate.sh.

agents_completed.description: Agents whose traces were observed at report-write time.

consistency_scan.description: Cross-artifact consistency scan outcome (#1024 / R3 derivation).

agent_verdicts.description: Map<agent, verdict> derived from .runs/agent-traces/*.json — the RAW (pre-fix) verdict each Phase-1 agent emitted. check-cross-artifact-consistency.py cross-checks this map against actual trace verdicts. For post-fix state, consumers should read agent_verdicts_after_fixes.

agent_verdicts_after_fixes.description: Map<agent, verdict> reflecting POST-FIX state. Derived without re-running Phase-1 agents: for security-defender/security-attacker → security-fixer.json.unresolved_critical (gated on AOC v1.1 trace validity); for accessibility-scanner/design-consistency-checker → quality-fixer.json.unresolved_critical; for ux-journeyer → unresolved_dead_ends on its own trace; for design-critic and others → raw verdict unchanged. Closes #1151.

agent_verdicts_after_fixes_source.description: Map<agent, source-string> documenting where each after-fix verdict was derived from (e.g., 'security-fixer.json', 'self', 'self (fixer absent)'). Companion to agent_verdicts_after_fixes for auditability.

hard_gate_failure.description: True iff any hard_gate predicate fired without an explicit acknowledgement. Consumed by lifecycle-finalize.sh, write-q-score.py, validate-recovery.sh.

process_violation.description: True iff process compliance failed (e.g., manifest agent skipped). Consumed by lifecycle-finalize.sh, write-q-score.py, verify-pr-gate.sh.

overall_verdict.description: Derived from gates only: 'pass' iff hard_gate_failure is false AND process_violation is false. NOT derived from agent_verdicts (which are pre-fix raw values). For post-fix consistency, see the R4 sub-rule that BLOCKS when overall_verdict=pass with any hard_gate agent in agent_verdicts_after_fixes=fail (warns on count_summary agents).
